other_observers: Joey Kellner; found by me, identified by the two of us.
description: the bird was a larid a bit smaller than a Ring-billed Gull. The overall shape was similar to a Ring-billed Gull, but with proportionally longer and more slender wings. The tail was also a bit longer, and the overall shape was more slender than that typical of a gull. The bill appeared quite small, though given the distance of the observation this was hard to discern for certain.  The overall color of the bird was dark choclatey brown. The underwings had some white checking and a large white flash at the base of the primaries. I could not see any white at all on the upperwing; given the distance I would not expect to be able to discern white primary shafts.  The uppertail coverts had some white checking; given the distance I think the white checking must have been at least somewhat extensive to be visible. The tail was slightly wedge shaped, and the two central tail feathers stuck out a little bit.Behaviors: The bird flew with a buoyant, tern like flight; the overall impression of the bird, both on the water and in the air, was of a slender, small jaeger.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenal dark phase
